title: Fix the Incorrect Trie Node Inserter

statement.markdown: You are given a Trie data structure implemented as a JavaScript object. Implement a function called insertNode that inserts a new word into the Trie. The function should handle the case where the word already exists in the Trie. The function should return `true` if the word was inserted successfully and `false` if it was not. However, there is a bug in the implementation that causes it to return `true` even if the word is not inserted successfully.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a string representing the word to be inserted into the Trie.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return a boolean indicating whether the word was inserted successfully.
statement.notes: The Trie data structure is implemented as a JavaScript object with a root node. The root node has a property called `children` that is an object containing the child nodes. Each child node has a property called `word` that is the word stored in that node.

constraints.description: The number of words in the Trie
The word to be inserted into the Trie

examples.explanation: The word 'hello' is inserted into the Trie
The word 'world' is inserted into the Trie
The word 'hello' already exists in the Trie and is not inserted again

hints.text: The Trie data structure is implemented as a JavaScript object with a root node.
The root node has a property called `children` that is an object containing the child nodes.

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing the Incorrect Trie Node Inserter
editorial.intuition: The Trie data structure is a tree-like data structure where each node has a value and a set of child nodes. The root node has a property called `children` that is an object containing the child nodes.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would be to iterate through the Trie and insert the word at each position. However, this approach has a time complexity of O(n), where n is the number of words in the Trie.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would be to use a recursive function that inserts the word into the Trie by traversing the child nodes.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a recursive function that inserts the word into the Trie by traversing the child nodes. The function should return `true` if the word is inserted successfully and `false` if it is not.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven by showing that the recursive function correctly inserts the word into the Trie and returns the correct boolean value.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the solution would involve inserting a word into the Trie and verifying that the function returns the correct boolean value.
commonMistakes: Not handling the case where the word already exists in the Trie
Not returning the correct boolean value
